subject: Sociology
title: Micro vs Macro Sociology
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Micro vs Macro Sociology.

hook.text: Two sociologists watch a coffee shop. One studies the conversations. The other studies who can afford to be there.

buildup.text: Micro sociology examines small-scale interactions: body language, conversations, rituals between individuals.

discovery.text: Macro sociology zooms out to institutions, economies, and power structures that shape entire populations.

twist.text: They're not rivals. Your job interview (micro) is shaped by the labor market (macro). Both levels connect.

climax.text: Good sociology moves between scales, showing how face-to-face moments reflect and reproduce larger systems.

punchline.text: Small interactions carry the fingerprints of big structures.

quiz.question: What does micro sociology focus on?
